vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1642: Stream conversation view + Streamdown renderer (flag) + fix markdown table rendering

plan.title: Stream conversation view + Streamdown renderer (flag) + fix markdown table rendering
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Three related dashboard issues: (1) the conversation view only shows messages after a turn finishes — long multi-tool turns leave the user staring at nothing; (2) we want an opt-in Streamdown markdown renderer for AI-streaming-friendly rendering; (3) chat markdown tables squish neighboring columns into ragged wraps when one cell holds a long unbreakable token (URL/branch/SHA).
narratives.Proposal: Part 1: the server already streams incremental message-level updates via the `subscribeConversationMessages` RPC (`watchConversation` fs.watch + byte-offset tail) but the frontend never consumes it — wire the conversation panel to that subscription for live claude-code conversations, keep HTTP fetch/poll for ended and pi/codex conversations, and add a server guard so Pi files are never mis-parsed. Part 2: add an `experimental.streamdownRenderer` flag (default off) threaded through config-yaml.ts + settings-api.ts + the frontend SettingsConfig, a Settings toggle mirroring the claudeCodeChannels toggle, and a Streamdown render path in ChatMarkdown that reuses our existing components/remarkPlugins/urlTransform and matches the dashboard theme. Part 3: CSS-only fix (github-markdown-css `display:block; width:max-content; min-width:100%; overflow-x:auto`) to both `.chatMarkdown` and `.markdownContent` tables so wide tables scroll instead of deforming.

autoDecisions.summary: Consume the existing server `subscribeConversationMessages` WS subscription rather than building a new JSONL tailer.
autoDecisions.rationale: The server already implements fs.watch + byte-offset incremental tail and pushes ConversationEvent over /ws/rpc; the contract and handler exist. The only gap is that the frontend has zero consumers and still 2s-HTTP-polls. Reusing it is less code and avoids duplicating the watch/parse logic.

autoDecisions.summary: Gate streaming to harness==='claude-code' && sessionAlive; keep current HTTP path for pi/codex/ended conversations.
autoDecisions.rationale: The watch/parse path is Claude-JSONL-specific and the Pi parser has no incremental support. Gating on the existing conversation.harness field satisfies the 'degrade gracefully' acceptance criterion with no Pi-parser changes.

autoDecisions.summary: Implement Part 3 as CSS-only via display:block on the table, not a <table> component wrapper.
autoDecisions.rationale: github-markdown-css uses exactly this; `display:block` turns the table into its own horizontal scroll container, fixing the squish for both renderers without touching ChatMarkdown component code. Simpler and lower-risk than wrapping every table in a div.

autoDecisions.summary: Add the Streamdown flag end-to-end through the explicitly-enumerated config sites and default it off.
autoDecisions.rationale: Neither config-yaml.ts nor settings-api.ts accepts arbitrary experimental keys — they enumerate each flag for load/save/validate. Mirroring claudeCodeChannels is the established, lint-clean pattern and ensures the flag actually round-trips.

narrative.Action: Add a frontend hook (e.g. useConversationMessagesStream) that uses WsTransport.subscribe() to consume the existing `subscribeConversationMessages` RPC (packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts; method pan.subscribeConversationMessages). On each ConversationEvent { kind:'messages', ... } payload, write the result into the SAME react-query cache key used today (['conversation-messages', name]) via queryClient.setQueryData so existing consumers update without polling. Handle the initial-state emit (handler parses from offset 0), the 'discovering' kind, reconnect (WsTransport auto-reconnects and the handler re-emits from 0), and clean teardown on unmount. Include a code comment documenting that this is message-level streaming only — the JSONL has no token deltas (decision D4).

subItems.title: Hook subscribes via WsTransport and merges ConversationEvent payloads into the ['conversation-messages', name] query cache
Subscription tears down on unmount / when conversation name changes; no leaked subscriptions across conversation switches
On WS reconnect the hook re-receives full initial state (no stale/empty render)
Code comment documents message-level (not token-level) streaming and why

items.title: Wire ConversationPanel + ConversationView to stream for live claude-code conversations

narrative.Action: In ConversationPanel.tsx (header query ~:174) and the inner ConversationView (~:1073), use the stream hook when conversation.harness === 'claude-code' && conversation.sessionAlive. When streaming, disable the 2s refetchInterval (both components read the same cache key, fed by the hook — hazard H2). For pi/codex/unknown harness and ended conversations, keep the current behavior unchanged (one-shot fetch for ended; 2s poll for live non-claude). Ensure both consumers share one subscription, not two.

subItems.title: Viewing a live claude-code conversation shows new assistant messages / tool calls / tool results incrementally with no manual refresh
Completed/historical conversations still load fully (no regression); no polling left running after a session ends
Pi/codex conversations render correctly via the existing path (no corrupted/blank messages)
ConversationPanel header spinner and ConversationView body stay consistent (single shared subscription, no duplicate fetch fighting the cache)

items.title: Server guard: subscribeConversationMessages must never mis-parse Pi session files

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts subscribeConversationMessages (~:590), detect Pi session files via isPiSessionFile (defined in routes/conversations.ts:568 — reuse/export it) before invoking watchConversation. For Pi files, emit a single 'discovering'/snapshot event and do NOT start the Claude-only watch (which would emit garbage). Defense-in-depth behind the frontend gating (D2/D3).

subItems.title: subscribeConversationMessages does not start a Claude watch for a Pi session file
No Claude-parsed messages are emitted for a Pi conversation through the subscription

items.title: Add experimental.streamdownRenderer flag end-to-end (config + settings API + frontend type)

narrative.Action: Thread a new boolean `experimental.streamdownRenderer` (default false) through every enumerated site, mirroring claudeCodeChannels: src/lib/config-yaml.ts (ExperimentalConfig interface, NormalizedExperimentalConfig, defaults, merge logic) and src/lib/settings-api.ts (ApiSettingsConfig type ~209, loadSettingsApi normalization ~613, save serialization ~780, validateSettings ~983) and the frontend Settings/types.ts experimental block (:174). Add a settings round-trip test asserting the flag persists.

subItems.title: GET /api/settings returns experimental.streamdownRenderer (default false) and a save persists a true value across reload
validateSettings rejects a non-boolean streamdownRenderer; frontend SettingsConfig type includes the field

items.title: Add the streamdown dependency to the frontend workspace

narrative.Action: Run `bun add streamdown` in src/dashboard/frontend (pinned version) so it appears in src/dashboard/frontend/package.json and bun.lock. Do not wire it up yet — just install. Verify the frontend still builds.

subItems.title: streamdown is a pinned dependency in src/dashboard/frontend/package.json and bun.lock
npm run build (frontend) still succeeds with the dependency added

items.title: Add the Streamdown experimental toggle to SettingsPage

narrative.Action: In SettingsPage.tsx Experimental section (~:3260), add a toggle row mirroring the Claude Code Channels toggle, with a handleStreamdownToggle handler mirroring handleClaudeCodeChannelsToggle (~:1169). Default off. Give it a data-testid (e.g. experimental-streamdown-toggle) and clear label/description ('Render chat markdown with Streamdown — research preview').

subItems.title: Experimental section shows a Streamdown toggle, default off, that persists via saveMutation like the other experimental toggles
Toggle has an aria role=switch + data-testid for UAT and reflects the persisted value on reload

items.title: Render chat markdown via Streamdown when the flag is on, preserving all customizations

narrative.Action: In ChatMarkdown.tsx, read the persisted experimental.streamdownRenderer flag (via the same ['settings'] query used by LowCostModePill.tsx). When ON, render via Streamdown; when OFF, keep the current ReactMarkdown path unchanged (default off). Pass through our existing customizations: the `components` map (file-link chip `a` override + CodeBlock `pre` override), `remarkPlugins` (remarkGfm + remarkBareFileTextLinks), and the transformMarkdownUrl URL transform incl. javascript:/data:/vbscript: blocking. Verify Streamdown's built-in rehype-harden / urlTransform does not strip file-link chip hrefs (shouldPreserveMarkdownFileLinkHref) and introduces no new XSS surface. Keep the ChatMarkdownErrorBoundary wrapper.

subItems.title: Flag on → chat renders via Streamdown; flag off → byte-identical to current ReactMarkdown renderer
Under Streamdown: file-link chips, bare-path autolinks, and issue/PR links all still work
Under Streamdown: URL sanitization holds — javascript:/data:/vbscript: hrefs are neutralized; no new XSS surface from model output
Code blocks still render with highlighting/copy; error boundary still catches render failures

items.title: Wire Streamdown to the dashboard theme tokens (light/dark)

narrative.Action: Streamdown is Tailwind + CSS-custom-property themed. Map its color/border/background custom properties to our existing tokens (--background, --foreground, --border, --accent, --card, etc.) so Streamdown output visually matches the dashboard in both light and dark mode. Add the necessary CSS in command-deck.module.css (or a scoped wrapper class) alongside the existing .chatMarkdown styles.

subItems.title: With the flag on, Streamdown text/links/tables/code match the dashboard theme in light mode
With the flag on, Streamdown output matches the dashboard theme in dark mode

items.title: Fix markdown table squish in both renderers (CSS-only horizontal scroll)

narrative.Action: In command-deck.module.css apply the github-markdown-css table pattern to BOTH `.chatMarkdown table` (~:3499) and `.markdownContent table` (~:1725): set `display:block; width:max-content; min-width:100%; overflow-x:auto;` so a cell with a long unbreakable token (URL/branch/SHA) scrolls horizontally instead of stealing width from neighboring columns. Keep the existing border/padding/zebra styling intact. This is independent of the Streamdown flag (Streamdown handles its own tables).

subItems.title: A table whose cell holds a long URL/branch/SHA scrolls horizontally and no longer squishes neighboring columns, in both .chatMarkdown and .markdownContent
Normal small tables still render tidy and fill the row width (min-width:100%)
